description: Replace sparseimage-backed devdrive with native APFS volumes, FSKit overlay namespace, and self-correcting reconcile loop

userStories.title: Pressure observers — read-only disk state watchers
userStories.description: Implement 4 read-only observers (purgeable_watch, clang_cache_watch, band_bloat_watch, docker_watch) that sample disk state every 60s and record to a ring buffer. No repair actions — pure observation.
acceptanceCriteria: observers.py contains PurgeableWatch, ClangCacheWatch, BandBloatWatch, DockerWatch classes
Each observer implements observe() -> dict with metrics (free_gb, purgeable_gb, cache_mb, etc.)
Ring buffer stores 288 samples (24h at 5-min resolution) in devdrive_state.json
CLI: lfg devdrive pressure prints current snapshot
CLI: lfg devdrive history prints tabular ring buffer with labeled events
Tests pass for all 4 observers with mock diskutil/df output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reconcile loop — dry-run drift classifier
userStories.description: Implement the reconcile loop that classifies 8 drift categories (MISSING_LINK, STALE_TARGET, REAL_DIR_DRIFT, UNMOUNTED_VOL, OVERSIZED, BAND_BLOAT, SNAPSHOT_LOCKED, SWAP_PRESSURE) with NO repair actions — dry-run only. Runs via LaunchAgent every 5 min.
acceptanceCriteria: reconcile.py implements ReconcileLoop with classify_drift() -> list[DriftEvent]
8 drift category enum with detection logic for each
CLI: lfg devdrive reconcile runs one cycle, prints classified drift
CLI: lfg devdrive doctor shows combined pressure + drift report
io.lfg.devdrive-reconcile.plist LaunchAgent template created (not installed)
Reconcile results logged to reconcile_log.jsonl (append-only)
Tests pass for each drift category with mock filesystem state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reconcile actions — idempotent repair with confirmation
userStories.description: Implement repair actions for each drift category. Each action is idempotent and requires explicit --apply flag. Actions check for active processes (lsof) before relocating.
acceptanceCriteria: actions.py implements fix_missing_link, fix_stale_target, fix_real_dir_drift, fix_unmounted_vol, fix_band_bloat, fix_snapshot_locked
Every action has dry-run mode that is the default
CLI: lfg devdrive reconcile --apply executes classified repairs
fix_real_dir_drift uses rsync + checksum verify before swapping symlink
fix_band_bloat checks mount state and idle time before compacting
lsof check prevents relocation of dirs with open file handles
APM notification emitted for each non-trivial action
Tests pass for each action with mock filesystem
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Native APFS volume backend — create, delete, quota, snapshot
userStories.description: Implement apfs_volume.py that wraps diskutil apfs commands for creating volumes in the root container with quota/reserve, deleting volumes, renaming, adjusting quotas, and taking/listing snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: apfs_volume.py implements create_volume(name, quota_gb, reserve_gb), delete_volume(name), rename_volume(old, new), set_quota(name, gb), list_snapshots(name), take_snapshot(name)
create_volume calls diskutil apfs addVolume on root container with -quota and -role U
All operations validate preconditions (container has space, volume exists, etc.)
CLI: lfg devdrive adopt <path> creates APFS volume and relocates
Error handling: returns structured result dict, never raises for user-facing operations
Tests pass with mock diskutil output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: v1-to-v2 migration — sparseimage to APFS volume
userStories.description: Implement per-volume migration from sparseimage to native APFS volume: preflight checks, rsync with progress, shasum verification, mount-point preservation, 24h rollback window.
acceptanceCriteria: migrate_v1.py implements preflight(volume_name), migrate(volume_name, dry_run=True), rollback(volume_name), cleanup(volume_name)
preflight checks: container capacity, image currently attached, writes manifest checksum
migrate: creates APFS vol with -v2 suffix, rsync -aHAX, shasum sample verify, detach old, rename new to original mount point
rollback: re-attach old sparseimage to shadow mount, rsync back
CLI: lfg devdrive migrate v1-to-v2 walks user through each volume (smallest first)
CLI: lfg devdrive migrate rollback <vol> reverses a single migration
24h grace period before sparseimage deletion (tracked in state file)
APM notification for each migration step
Tests pass with mock diskutil/hdiutil
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LaunchAgent install and reboot verification
userStories.description: Install the reconcile LaunchAgent, verify APFS volumes auto-mount at boot (no automount plist needed), remove legacy automount plist after confirming.
acceptanceCriteria: io.lfg.devdrive-reconcile.plist installed to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
launchctl load/bootstrap loads the agent successfully
Reconcile loop runs every 5 minutes and logs to reconcile_log.jsonl
Verification script confirms APFS volumes mount at boot (checks mount table post-reboot)
Legacy io.lfg.devdrive-automount.plist preserved during migration, removed only after reconcile runs successfully once post-reboot
CLI: lfg devdrive setup --reconcile installs and starts the agent
Tests: agent plist validates with plutil
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: FSKit overlay exploration — macFUSE 5.2 proof of concept
userStories.description: Build a proof-of-concept FSKit overlay filesystem using macFUSE 5.2's FSKit backend that presents a unified /Developer namespace routing to underlying APFS volumes based on config rules. This is exploratory — validate feasibility before committing.
acceptanceCriteria: Python FUSE binding (fusepy or llfuse) installed and working with macFUSE 5.2 FSKit backend
Proof-of-concept: mount at /tmp/devdrive-overlay that routes reads/writes to /Volumes/DDRV<N> based on path rules
Routing config in devdrive_state.json maps path prefixes to volumes
Basic operations work: ls, cat, mkdir, write, stat through the overlay
Performance measurement: compare latency of overlay vs direct volume access for 1000 file operations
Document findings: feasibility, performance delta, stability, macOS integration gotchas
Go/no-go decision documented for full FSKit integration
Typecheck passes (Python only — no Swift FSKit yet)

userStories.title: State file v2 and devdrive_state.json schema
userStories.description: Define and implement the v2 state file schema (devdrive_state.json) that replaces devdrive_forest.json with volume inventory, forest topology, pressure metrics ring buffer, and reconcile history.
acceptanceCriteria: devdrive_state.json schema documented with version field, volumes array, forest array, metrics ring buffer
State migration: reads existing devdrive_forest.json, converts to v2 format, writes devdrive_state.json
Volume entries include: name, mount_point, device, quota_bytes, used_bytes, role, health, last_reconcile, migrated_from
Forest entries include: id, system_path, volume, target, expected_kind, last_observed_kind, drift_count, auto_repair
Metrics ring buffer: 288 samples with ts, df_free_gb, container_free_gb, purgeable_gb
All modules (observers, reconcile, actions) read/write through state module API
Tests pass for schema validation and migration
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Menubar pressure indicator and history graph
userStories.description: Add a DevDrive pressure section to the LFG menubar helper showing current df_free with color-coded threshold. Click opens history graph in WebKit viewer.
acceptanceCriteria: Menubar shows disk free with color: green (>10GB), yellow (5-10GB), red (<5GB)
Click on pressure indicator opens WebKit viewer with D3.js time-series chart
Chart shows last 24h of ring buffer data: df_free_gb, container_free_gb, purgeable_gb
Pressure events (SNAPSHOT_LOCKED, etc.) annotated as vertical markers on chart
Menubar refreshes every 60s from devdrive_state.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Claude Code integration — eliminate symlink dependency
userStories.description: After migration, ensure ~/.claude/projects and ~/.claude/tasks point to native APFS volume mount points (not sparseimage-backed paths). Verify mkdir -p works reliably for all project memory directories.
acceptanceCriteria: ~/.claude/projects and ~/.claude/tasks symlinks point to APFS volume paths
mkdir -p succeeds for all existing project memory directories
New Claude Code sessions create memory dirs without errors
Reconcile loop monitors ~/.claude symlinks as forest entries with auto_repair: true
If FSKit overlay is approved (US-007): paths resolve through overlay instead of symlinks
Typecheck passes
